figures.caption: Table 2. Tooth detection on PARTHENON: ⋆baseline YOLOv8; †enhanced with P2, BiFPN, WIoU.

Fig. 1. Quantitative results on PARTHENON: (a) MATHE variants outperform baseline models in tooth detection (mAP50); (b, c) MATHENA variants show superior Dice scores in CarSeg and AD. (†: baseline enhanced with P2, BiFPN, and WIoU).
Table 3. Multi-task performance on PARTHENON: CarSeg and AD averaged across subsets; DDS on DDSNet.
Table 1. PARTHENON composition: ①-④ denote tooth detection, Caries Segmentation (CarSeg), Anomaly Detection (AD), and Dental Developmental Staging (DDS).
Table 4. Ablation study on PARTHENON: impact of each component across all tasks.
Fig. 3. Visual comparison: Left shows Tooth Detection; Right depicts CarSeg.

Fig. 2. MATHENA architecture: Left shows MATHE backbone, BiFPN, and detection head; Right depicts HENA encoder-decoder with GCST skip fusion. SRA (Spatial ReAlignment) remaps the output mask to the original OPG for visualization.
